file_insights.changes: Created comprehensive serializers for authentication and user management (528 lines)
key_components: LoginSerializer with credential validation and token generation
file_insights.patterns: DRF serializer best practices with comprehensive validation and error handling

file_insights.changes: Added JWT authentication views (login, logout, refresh, password change)
key_components: LoginView - handles user authentication with email/password
file_insights.patterns: Class-based views with proper permission classes and authentication backends

file_insights.changes: Added URL routing for authentication endpoints

key_settings: JWT_AUTH_COOKIE configuration for secure token storage

file_insights.pattern: Routed /api/auth/ to core app authentication urls

file_insights.changes: Exported ErrorCodes for use across application

patterns_discovered.description: LoginSerializer validates user existence, soft-delete status, user status (SUSPENDED/INVITED), password correctness, and organization status before generating tokens
Both LoginSerializer and CustomTokenObtainPairSerializer add custom claims (email, names, organization info) to refresh and access tokens for frontend consumption
PasswordChangeSerializer revokes all active sessions when password is changed, forcing re-authentication across all devices
LogoutSerializer uses SimpleJWT's token.blacklist() to invalidate refresh tokens immediately
Response serializers nest UserSerializer and OrganizationMinimalSerializer for complete context in auth responses
All validation errors use ErrorCodes enum for consistent error handling across API
Login flow checks is_deleted flag and status enum before allowing authentication
Login validates organization status (SUSPENDED/DELETED) to prevent access if org is compromised

gotchas_discovered.detail: LoginSerializer normalizes email to lowercase and strips whitespace to prevent case-sensitivity issues with database lookups

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Session serializer placeholder for current session detection
gotchas_discovered.detail: SessionSerializer.get_is_current() returns False as placeholder - requires session ID from token claims to properly identify current session

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Custom claims added at different levels
gotchas_discovered.detail: Custom claims added in LoginSerializer for response AND in CustomTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() - need to ensure consistency
LogoutSerializer blacklists token immediately but frontend must still handle token in local storage - race conditions possible

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Organization relationship not enforced at serializer level
gotchas_discovered.detail: LoginSerializer allows None organization but organization-level checks still execute - potential for edge cases with optional relationships
Check for is_deleted flag on User model happens before status check - order matters for error message precedence
PasswordChangeSerializer has min_length=12 hardcoded - should be configurable setting for policy changes

approach_outcome.summary: Implemented complete JWT authentication system with login, logout, token refresh, and password change views with comprehensive validation and security checks
key_achievements: Multi-layer validation in LoginSerializer covering user state, organization state, and credentials
Session revocation on password change for security
Custom JWT claims for frontend context
Comprehensive error handling with specific error codes
RESTful endpoint structure with proper HTTP methods

recommendations.recommendation: Implement current session detection by storing session ID in JWT token claims and using it in get_is_current() method
Extract hardcoded values (12-char password minimum, token expiration times) to environment variables or settings for runtime configuration
Add rate limiting to login endpoint to prevent brute force attacks (use django-ratelimit or similar)
Implement email verification requirement in LoginSerializer - add flag check similar to UserStatus checks
Add audit logging for authentication events (login, logout, failed attempts, password changes)
Consider implementing sliding window for token refresh (auto-refresh on activity) to improve UX
Add request/response examples in docstrings for each view endpoint
Consider separating response serializers into dedicated file if codebase grows further
